## Summary

Extend **`score-registry`** so lenders can reject **stale on-chain attestations**. Today `updated_at` is stored but consumers must trust that an old tier A attestation still reflects current off-chain score.

---

## Problem

A user attests tier A on-chain once, then their off-chain ZCore score drops — on-chain record remains tier A indefinitely. DeFi protocols reading `get_score` cannot enforce recency without custom logic.

---

## Proposed contract changes

**File:** `Contracts/score-registry/src/lib.rs` (v2 or upgrade)

### Extended storage

```rust
pub struct ScoreRecord {
    pub score: u32,
    pub tier: u32,
    pub updated_at: u64,
    pub valid_until: u64,  // ledger timestamp; 0 = no expiry
}
```

### New / updated functions

```rust
/// Oracle sets TTL when writing (e.g. now + 30 days)
pub fn set_score(env, wallet, score, tier, ttl_secs: u64)

/// Public: is record within max_age of current ledger time?
pub fn is_score_fresh(env, wallet, max_age_secs: u64) -> bool

/// Returns record if fresh; else default REJECTED (score 0, tier 0)
pub fn get_score_if_fresh(env, wallet, max_age_secs: u64) -> ScoreRecord
```

### Recommended TTL policy (document, not hardcode)

| Tier | Suggested TTL |
|------|---------------|
| A | 30 days |
| B | 21 days |
| C | 14 days |
| REJECTED | 7 days |

Oracle computes `valid_until = env.ledger().timestamp() + ttl_secs` in `set_score`.

---

## Server changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `Server/src/services/soroban.service.ts` | Pass TTL to contract; parse `valid_until` on read |
| `Server/src/controllers/contracts.controller.ts` | Include `validUntil` in API response |
| `Front/app/dashboard/attestation/page.tsx` | Show expiry date; warn if stale |

---

## Migration strategy

- Deploy **new contract** (safer than upgrading uninitialized instances)
- Update `SCORE_REGISTRY_CONTRACT_ID` in Vercel
- Re-attest active users or run batch (#26)

---

## Tests

- [ ] Fresh record: `is_score_fresh` → true
- [ ] Expired record: `get_score_if_fresh` → zeros
- [ ] TTL=0 disables expiry (backward compat mode)

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Contract deployed testnet with freshness fields
- [ ] Unit tests pass
- [ ] API exposes `validUntil`
- [ ] `Contracts/README.md` documents TTL recommendations
- [ ] Gatekeeper (#28) uses `get_score_if_fresh`
